reviews.review_description:
Overall, I like the game quite a bit, especially the multiplayer, which is a lot of fun. However, everything is quite monotonous and a boring gray on gray, which occasionally veers towards green. The weapon movement also lags, which urgently needs to be optimized, as the gameplay isn't fluid. Highly recommended for two players! There are also many different types of zombies and the missions are very long!

Update: They removed the multiplayer—the only reason anyone bought this game in the first place. Why is this just allowed to happen? I want a refund!

I already thought the regular Trilogy was really cool, but I think Zombie Army VR is even better. I’ve already played through it once and am now replaying all the chapters to find every item.  That means I’m having a ton of fun with the VR version. It has a nice, dark atmosphere and some really cool shooting action. The VR mechanics, like manually reloading weapons, work perfectly and are always reliable. In my opinion, that’s still not standard in other VR shooters. Oh, and the typical collectibles like the dolls and the funny creepy hands made it into this version too. The only downside is that the campaign is a bit short. I’m not interested in the co-op mode, so that doesn’t change my rating. Besides, I don’t think it’s necessary for this game anyway. So I’m giving it five stars.

That was the main reason I bought it. Not many similar style games that have co-op option (without having to be a military gun expert). I’d return/refund it if we hadn’t played it so much and had it since the first day.

For some reason around the 2nd or 3rd chapter while I was playing with my friend, picking up ammo suddenly didnt give me any ammo, which is a big problem for me, and if you have any ways to fix it then please let me know, otherwise it’s a good game
Edit: changed from 3 stars down to 1 star
It used to be super good and super fun to play with your friends and hunt zombies together, but now with the coop removed, its just become stale.
Just play it for 1.5 hours and refund it, not worth it anymore at all
Co op was the only thing that made me play this game at all. Since that option is no longer available,  this game will just sit in my library collecting dust. Waste of time and money.

Had a lot of hope for this game and while at first I had some fun I was quickly left disapointed by the story, the graphics which are seiously terrible and the weapons which feel super lame (no real weight or recoil and horrible reloading not to even mention the models themselves). I had a lot of hope for this game but in the end it was just a serious waste of money. I really regret buying this.
The worst part by far is how the devs just took all of our money and just bailed on this game. Feels like they are just spitting in our faces at this point.

So first I will say it is lacking 2 hands on a handgun. However; now I gotta say this game is the way Sniper Elite should have been made. So off you go redo fix it with this new group. 
    This is finally actually good zombie game, that after watch videos, on this game, at previous game shows, was not impressive at all. Actually created their own bad reviews. So I’m changing my review, as now I can see a lot of effort went into this, and you have my full respect. Thank you for delivering a great Zombie Army to VR.
     UPDATED. I have never been so disappointed in a company, as I am with Rebellion, after refusing to fix issues, and then rage quitting, like a bunch of a offended want to be school girls, chasing rainbows. The multiplayer only ever needed to add in those snip cams, but instead the abandon it, cause they are too petty to take criticism

reviews.review_title: Bought for co-op, rating for no co-op
reviews.review_description: I bought this game mainly to play a co-op VR game with my son. Now that co-op has been removed, I have no motivation to play the game. It’s another piece of digital hoarding.  I’ll uninstall from all our devices and it will sit in our archive, not getting our money’s worth.￼

I play this with my husband. We love this game. There were some issues in the beginning, and there are still some disconnect issues. But this has become my favorite game. I would love some add ons so i can keep playing. .  We will clear our data so we can play fresh all over again.
So sad that co op was taken away. This WAS our favorite game.

Love this game. A zombie slaying adventure that’s absolute chaos and joy… Earlier issues with network connection errors seem to have been addressed. It rarely happens now. 

Edit: They removed coop. The main reason I bought this game was to play with a friend. Liked it so much we both purchased the PCVR version too then only for them to kill coop. I really only play multiplayer games in VR.

Game is officially broken 😡 
Co-op is dead! ☠️ 
Will try asking Meta for credit.
Tossing us a measly skins to compensate is unacceptable.
It is a really good game

Loved every minute of this game. Once you’re used to the various different types of grenades and what they do you have a blast. Hope there’s a sequel.. If you enjoyed Saints & Sinners and Arizona sunshine you will love this…. I’m running out of zombie shooters now
Abandoning your baby already is nothing less than sad
Good campaign but I’m sad about co-op.

Can't tell you how many times I've restarted this game cause it just loses my place and decides I have to start over.
I don't have this problem with any other game I have on Quest 3.
This game has always glitched on me. I cannot accept invites or invite  during game play with my husband. It’s honestly ridiculous. This game is ok if it’s single player. But glitches for multiplayer

final update: 
Almost half a year has passed, and the developers haven't even bothered to address the problem with the hands' framerate, So I've lost all hope that they'll ever get to fix it, don't even bother buying it unless you want to experience one of the most annoying and eye strainer bugs you can find in VR, save your money or get another game

Old review:
It's been two months already. How is it possible you haven't fixed a bug that affects the entire game?
The hands still move at a significantly lower frame rate than the rest of the world.

reviews.review_title: The undead are fresher after updates!
reviews.review_description:
Updated Review:

It's Halloween and I'm looking for more spooky games to play so I took a chance and repurchased this as it's had a few patches since I got a refund. (I say "took a chance" since I don't believe that Meta will refund you twice for the same game.)

Luckily aside from a soft lock in the initial training area, possibly caused by using the old save from when I first purchased it months ago, it's been smooth sailing and I'm throughly enjoying it so far.

It still looks like a Quest 2 game but the graphics have been polished up a bit and the underlying art direction is solid. Lowering the brightness and boosting the contrast in the game settings gets rid of the washed out look. The performance is excellent, it runs silky smooth.

There are some nice atmospheric effects like the layers of thick fog rolling through the battlefield.

Aside from not being able to use a two handed grip with the pistol the guns handle very well. The rifle scope is one of the best I've used in VR.

This is the perfect time of the year for a zombie game.

bHaptics support is a real plus also!

Original Review:

I'd enjoy this game enough to keep it if it was stable, but it crashed twice in less than a half hour on my Quest 3 so I'm requesting a refund before I crash my way past the end of the two hour return window.

I'd really love the game if it actually was a Quest 3 game, (and didn't crash frequently of course).

Sadly it looks like an average Quest 2 game with washed out lighting, low resolution textures, and foveated rendering.

The Quest 3 will have been out for two years this October yet new, heavily hyped games like this are still just games for the discontinued Quest 2.

Why not scale down PCVR games to Quest 3 then scale down the Quest 3 versions to run on the Quest 2?  Why cripple the Quest 3 version in favor of supporting a discontinued system?  I suspect the answer is money. 

They're not getting my $30 yet.  Eventually I may buy it again if it's debugged and gets some Quest 3 enhancements.

This "buy our Quest 2 game for your Quest 3 and maybe if we feel like it we'll throw in some Quest 3 enhancements later" nonsense is getting old.

$500 for a what's primarily been a Quest 2 emulator with pancake lenses is a lot to pay.  Meta's going to have to do something to entice developers to support the Quest 3 if they want to sell me the inevitable Quest 4.
No fun to play at all. Buy Arizona Sunshine instead.

reviews.review_title: A game I wanted to love but couldn't
reviews.review_description: I really wanted to love this game as I like sniper based games and Rebellion has a history of great ones but there is just so much broken about this game that I just couldn't starting with the sniper mechanics when zoomed the scope movement is so sensitive I can barely hold the scope on target and miss more often than I hit the smallest movement is very exaggerated when using the other weapons the color pallet makes the front sight hard to see making sighting difficult also even with it supposedly designed to be single player the zombie swarms were hard to handle at least for me all in all while fun this game needs some improvements to be really great.
Very fun but i am a baby when it comes to horror so i need a friend to play with but other than that very fun
not worth 20 pound

I didn't know when I was waiting for a release that it was the same developers as the Sniper games. I love those games. Both of them. This one is great too! It's a long campaign. I had alot of fun with it. And replaying to find all the collectables.
It's a great game!

Great gameplay. 

Gunplay feels good. But sometimes the nature of the arena is getting blocked off can trap you and make you have a cheap death. 

 I’d really like to see a pump action shotgun, and a revolver. With the franchisees history of DLC I hope that they add more to the game without charging ￼￼. 
I feel like they should do that for this title. 

It doesn’t make any sense that you can’t manually close the shotguns that are brake action you can only do it by flicking your controller upward, which is cool, but it’s stupid that you can’t do it. The manual actual way.

Also for a zombie army game, the Gore is very lackluster. we should see blood spraying and body parts are falling apart. The zombies don’t really seem to get shot up as well as as in other zombie shooters

The sniping is fun overall this is a good game and I do recommend it. There are some things about it that make me wanting for More and I hope they find a way to.

9/10 from me. The movement is nice and smooth and relatively fast. Gameplay is well paced and just when you think it's too easy it ramps up towards the end of each level. Reloading each weapon is easy and very satisfying. Duel wield just about any combination of weapons you can think of! Buy this game NOW.
I was not sure what to expect from this game as I waited for its release. It was a little hard to get used to, but now I love this game would highly recommend. ￼

I’ve never played or had any interest in a Zombie Army game until this release and like many of us, I have been frustrated by the delays & disappointed by the early-access builds of this game. 

Even in the last couple of days, influencers and reviewers have received their copies and I thought ‘oh here we go, this really is going to be bad’ based on the lack of gameplay and context they were allowed to provide. 

I’m actually surprised and credit where credit is due, the game is very fun and the gameplay loop & progression system -which admittedly could do with some fleshing out and expanding upon- is VERY moreish. The weapon upgrade and skins system are also surprisingly fleshed out considering what we’ve come to expect from our VR games.

Edit- IGNORE anybody reviewing this game based on past footage or the fact this game isn’t 4 player co-op, the game’s extra development time has clearly paid off and it was never advertised as being a 4 player one!

Mine is the only opinion that matters, ya know. I don't even get why they let others post reviews... Anywho... I'm experienced with Zombie shooters enough to tell right from wrong, and in the WEEKS I've owned this game, I've been able to get on and play multiplayer exactly twice, on Day 1. Since then, it's been nonstop nothingness--like literally worthless--because multiplayer hasn't done anything since my first day. 

Honest to god, my George Foreman Grille saw more action, this past week...

Unfortunately, I played more than two hours, 'cause I've spent more than that trying to get it working(!), or I wouldn't officially own this, anymore...

There are a million Zombie Shooters out there. Heck, so much so that "Zombie Shooters" has become its own genre of video games -- if not THE largest sub-genre of any medium, ever. Honestly, there may be more Zombie Shooters than Roguelikes in the world, in mid-2025. 

VR has more than its share, too--pro'lly because they work so well with VR--to be sure, but not of the multiplayer variety, which is what we need, here, now -- and what was advertised for months. That's where this game has the *potential* to be good or even great. It's even fun in Switch -- or was, until VR shooters killed flatscreen shooters, for me... However, I can't abide an established dev muffing a game launch for a month. 

So... why would I care about this yet another player Zombie Shooter, while essentially bereft of multiplayer? 

After the Fall is a great game that, while showing its age, is still capable of smooth, four-player multiplayer--much more fun, from what I got to see, that day--despite having come out in late 2021 -- and it can go a couple hours on just the headset batteries.

At the very least, ZA:VR could offer credits, a $5 meta credit, a cosmetic (of desk work  Deadhunter, maybe, to capture the feeling of the game's loss) or at least *something* for jumping out with a big price on this "not ready for prime time players'" game. 

Thanks for reading, and hope this helps...

The game got bad reviews from a rough launch, but quickly fixed itself and it’s a fun experience. Environments are pretty varied, there’s good feeling guns that actually sound like guns, and how you interact with them is fairly customizable to how you handle them. Just know what you’re getting into; if you’re looking for a game mowing down hundreds of zombies at a time, this isn’t for you. In here, you’re fighting some 10-20 zombies at a time, but they’re a threat, as it slowly brings in different zombie classes that can make you adjust your strategies, the first variant having a gun, albeit floppy with their aim. 
Personally, I miss fighting against enemy classes, and not just dealing with a horde as your main threat. Don’t get me wrong, the zombies WILL horde you, but you better be listening if something starts screaming in the distance. Story wise, there’s one good enough to keep you playing but know that you’re here for the gameplay. 

I hope this game gets more recognition after it got review bombed after being buggy on release. I know it’s not great, but literally every game I’ve seen on this platform was bugged to hell on launch, it’s just how it goes these days. That and this game got fixed in like, less than a month and was fully playable for me, that’s the fastest I’ve seen it, so good job devs. I hope we can see that DLC come alive, but if it can’t, then I understand.
Not at all good full of bugs
Fun game and plays great 🤙🤙🤙

When this game was first announced it was advertised as a 4 player coop campaign. After several lengthy delays, they changed it to a 2 player campaign with a 4 player horde mode. Today is launch day, and there is no horde mode. The lobbies for 2 player coop appear to be down as well. This is a perfect case for a refund, but I thought I should at least leave a review first.

developer_response.body:
The game was never advertised as a 4-player co-op game, in fact during the whole of development it has only ever been 1-2 player.

In addition, there is a large-scale network outage affecting a lot of online services at the moment. We're getting updates on it and hopefully it won't be long. Poor timing that it's landed on launch day. Sorry about the inconvenience.

reviews.review_title: Gun mechanics Similar to sniper elite vr
reviews.review_description: Ok. I’m like 40% thru single player so I can leave a proper review.  I love it. It is fast paced with many zombies coming at u. You will feel overwhelmed but after respawn you’ll plot your strategy around ammo boxes and use your weapons to maximum benefit and when you clear to next check point feels worth the effort.  It has a story but that’s taken a back seat to simply staying alive.  It has teleport so motion sickness isn’t an issue.  Two big thumbs up from this guy. Go get ‘em dead hunter!
I am having a blast slaying zombies.  It's worth every penny.

Revised 06.22.2025

The Good:

- 3 Skill Levels
- Teleport Option
- Story Line
- Intro / Tutorial is pretty comprehensive
- Graphics are in the top 2% for a Quest 3 Game 
- Has Co-Op


Suggested Improvements & Bugs to Squash:

- I wish teleport was a bit less flashy. That brief black flash is a little nauseating. I have played games with no black flash and it makes a world of difference.
- Glitches! I can’t count how many times we’ve had to wonder around a level or restart it in the hopes that we could get a checkpoint to actually work and allow us to proceed through the game. So far, there’s at least one where we tried several times and could never get the starred lever to pull. The game will also have at least one “Network” error that will cause us to have to start over. We don’t have this issue with other games so it’s not a local issue. The game is definitely fun, but having to constantly restart and replay certain sections detracts from the experience.
- It would be helpful if there was a more obvious way to tell when/if the tutorial is over and the actual game is beginning. I kept going, waiting for a moment to be able to select Co-Op. Eventually I saw the option in The Hub, but that brings me to my next suggestion.
- The Quit and Return to the Hub button as as the Start Co-Op options should be single click. It took us awhile to figure out that we needed to hold down the trigger on these buttons to get them to work. 
- It would be great if players could teleport with both hands. I prefer to teleport with my right hand and don’t need the snap feature because I have a swiveling chair. 
- Personally, I’d prefer if there was an option to turn off friendly fire and self-harm (with explosives).
- I’d like to see more hand mirroring options. By this, I mean that I’d like to be able to do nearly everything with my left hand that the game allows me to do with my right hand. That said, you can do a lot.

Overall the game is pretty incredible. After an update or so, I wouldn’t be surprised if it becomes the number one game for Quest 3.

[I’ll add the 5th star after enough of these bugs are squashed]

developer_response.body: Appreciate the feedback, have passed this on to the team now. If these issues persist, please reach out to player support and quote the ref number 8699e1gxr: support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_description: If you thought you would be continuing the excellent gaming experience from Sniper Elite VR in this title, you were sadly mistaken. Although it may show some nicer graphics, Zombie Army VR's gameplay just feels dull and boring, way too repetive and without any further depth or fun with more advanced tactical possibilities maybe mixed with some open level building. Despite good graphics, its arcade style & mechanics do break the immersion too much. At its core it's nothing more than the "Cinema of the Dead" mod from Contractors and far away from other WWII themed Quest 3 top titles like Winter Warrior, MoH or Team Beaf's RtCW adaption. Sorry Rebellion but you really missed an opportunity here...

I'm liking the game so far. Feels like ZA. But for the love of God, give us an option to change the angle of the guns! I'm not Harry Potter, and I don't run a magic wand. My wrists won't take it. I will give you 5 stars if you fix this. 10 stars if you find a way to give us a more naturalistic in-game scope instead of just a jarring overlay, and fix the scale of the pistols (they're massive) and the zombies in kill-cam (they're ridiculously tiny.)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback. This will be passed onto the team.

reviews.review_title: Waiting for it, finally got it...
reviews.review_description: I'm not crazy far in the game at all and I've read the whiny reviews about small bugs and no 4 players (which they never said) but I have full confidence those will be fixed in a hop, skip, and a jump. They did a good job and I was worried from reviews I got from editorials months ago and they were proven wrong. Fun game. Good graphics, sounds, slow motion shots, etc. Congrats! Also, I apparently just moved it up to 4.0 so yes, yes!!!

reviews.review_title: Pretty solid for what it is.
reviews.review_description: Yeah there are some things I didn’t like about it such as not being able to two-hand pistols and the co-op bugs, but a well made campaign shooter is hard to find nowadays and this gets the job done.

Graphic should be better on quest 3, this is not how VR games should look like in 2025
Reloading guns is clunky sometimes
Weapon angle is way off, the pistol aims to much up, you need to turn your wrist down for 30° feels unnatural and uncomfortable, please make option for weapon angle adjustment.
Weapons look bad as well and the pistol is way to big, the scale is wrong. 
Fix this and its solid 4 Star zombie shooter.
I had some fun to be honest this game has potential.
Will update my review if game is polished.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback and suggestions. Will ensure this gets back to the team.

reviews.review_title: Can't recommend in its current state.
reviews.review_description: I've been a big fan of sniper elite games, as well as zombie army so I really wanted this to be good and want to like it. The gameplay seems fine though a bit buggy, which I could forgive. I cannot however forgive that the co-op is not working. I've tried with my friend hosting a room, and having them host and the same thing happens. Whoever is the host gets a message that says they have left the game. We can still hear each other but the game refuses to start in co-op. I have opened tickets for support, but the only responses I have recieved is asking me if I'm on quest or PCvr even though I've clearly stated it is a quest 3 problem. I'd say avoid at all costs until multiplayer works. I bought this game twice, once for me and a friend and we have spent more than 2 hours just trying to get co-op to work , I'm hoping I can get a refund because the game does not have working co-op as advertised.

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you're experiencing issues with cooperative play, but it's good you're already in direct contact with player support. They are best placed to resolve this issue for you and get to the bottom of the cause. The clarifying questions from player support are just so they can make sure they troubleshoot correctly, but I appreciate when you have a blocker like this it can be frustrating. Rest assured, they will work with you to resolve this.

reviews.review_description: First off i guess ppl cant read that tank a game in review cuzz it's not multi-player,  how dumb try learning to read it saids co op which means 2 players. Game is fantastic don't t listen to the dumb 2 and 3 star reviews talking about 4 players etc. Seriously fun in co op which is 2 players only duh lol. Update after 4 hrs game is fantastic,  graphics fantastic, ga.e mechanics solid easy using auto reload which is best for fast pace game like this, a action is intense with many survival trapped room shoot outs. Well done development team Bravo 👏 🙌.  A must buy for zombie lovers, we had a blast as we used some team strategy with our experience in co op  shooters and it worked.

The level design is alright, but the rest of the gameplay is buggy. Guns and explosives phasing through the floor never to be seen again. Crashed had no way to get my progress back there are no checkpoints throughout each mission, so if you crash, you have to do the mission again(each mission is around 30 min long). Now I have to do everything from the start. The melee doesn't feel good, and bullet hits are glitchy and delayed. Im forced to walk back and forth through a small area to prolong the length of each mission. The game is alright, although I dont think its worth the base price.

developer_response.body: If you are experiencing issues with the game, please speak with our player support team who should be able to troubleshoot these issues with you: support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_title: Ight, sit down, here's the issues I have
reviews.review_description:
Sniper scope - Just is not it. The amount of times I try to shoot a zombie without using the scope only for it to get in the way is insanely annoying. Why not just do the Into The Radius sniper scopes. Those worked well. 

Grips points - On the pistols, good, on the smgs, good, on some snipers, meh. The bolt-action snipers are very janky. When trying to grab the underside sometimes you'll accidentally grab the bolt. 

Sidearms - They are comically large and can't be two-handed. For some reason. Don't know what dingus thought that would be a good idea.

X-rays - The x-rays in this game are mid. Most of the time they zombies aren't even affected. The eyes and the sides of the head are like the only things that show damage, even when those areas aren't hit. And when the front of the head is hit only the sides are affected, DESPITE NOT BEING HIT. You can also clearly see the patches of bone on the cranium that are destructable. 

Hands - The fingers look kinda silly. The index finger on pretty much every character looks broken. This ones no big deal, just thought I'd mention it.

Melee - Yeah you guys shouldn't have bothered, sh!t is so obnoxiously janky.

reviews.review_title: A Good Idea But Very Repetitious
reviews.review_description: I wanted to like this game, but it is so repetitious it gets old fast.  It looks great, has a good premise, and there's a lot of potential here but the game gets bogged down in wave after wave of 360 degree Z's in confined locations at predictable points in the game.  Playing it in small sessions helps and there are in between sections where there is a bit more story.  I expect it will be fine for most but I've come to expect more than mindless shooting from games.

Please don’t give this game a bad review because it is not 4 player, it tells you in the description, some people are idiots.

So far so good, fun mechanics are just so good.
Worth the wait.

Was somewhat disappointed, I was so looking forward to this but after playing sniper elite vr and winter soldier,  I was expecting something much better than this. Its fun but after just  the first mission, I've decided that a refund is in order. I'll wait and pick this up again when it's on sale and after there's ( hopefully) been some updates.

this game is pretty awesome, its a pretty loud game getting progressively crazier as you go. beat it once, doing second playthrough on hard to upgrade all the weapons. one thing i really liked was the 80s zombie movie music. guns and controls are very responsive & function well. game would be too easy with a 4 player co-op like other people talked about. definately had a blast, and yeah make another one

My wife and I watched for this to release and waited patiently through the delay.  When it finally released,  we bought two so we could play together. 

Single player is pretty good, which is why we pulled the trigger on the 2nd copy.   Co-op is where the avalanche of bugs make the game almost unplayable.    There are really too many issues to list with co-op.

It starts with trying to sync up and goes downhill from there.   Throwables and whatnot falling through the floor.  Weapons with full ammo not firing.   Your hands will spontaneously float in front of your face and you can't grab anything.  The player animations and movements are a disaster.  I could keep going if the interwebs had more bandwidth.

Not really a bug, but an annoyance that vexes me.  We play in hard mode, and the health injections are generally worthless.   Two hits incapacitate you.  Even if you are carrying 3 shots.  You get incapacitated,  they swarm you.  Take one, immediately incapacitated again.  Take the second,  immediately incapacitated again.   Take the third,  start over from your checkpoint.  Unfortunately,  starting over from your checkpoint now seems like a blessing because we usually bug out.

I give single player a four.  Co-op barely a two.  Washes out at a weak three.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you are experiencing issues with cooperative play. If you are getting the following error '[ZA-434] Server version mismatch,' you simply need to update to the latest version of the game (Patch 2). 

If this is not the issue and cooperative play continues to be a persistent concern, please do reach out to our player support team so they can investigate this with you: https://support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_description:
Fun, hilarious, tense and satisfying and that was just in the first 5 minutes.   The first day I played for almost 4 hours total and had a great time.  This is going to the top of my all-time favorite zombie shooters.

I did finish the game after about 4 days of playing. Found it to be an amazing zombie shooter. Wave after wave location after location. Some claim it is "repetitive" well, you don't have to play the whole game in one sitting.  After finishing I have gone back in and played over and overlooking for all the collectables. 

I'll now go back and play in each of the different difficulty levels.

The ONLY thing I would change is to have the ability to switch the reload and the satchel buttons.  I use the reload every few minutes, but the satchel I rarely use. I end up accidently pressing the satchel button over and over my mistake as I play and it gets in the way.

Had very low expectations going into it from things I had heard, but the game is surprisingly well made and a lot of fun. It brings the fun of bobbing and weaving and lining up zombies to save ammo that you expect in the call of duty zombies or any other well made zombies game. Very awesome to have that experience in vr now. The graphics also were surprisingly good. If you’re on the fence about getting this game, get it (especially if you have a friend you can play with, as you can play the entire campaign with a friend online)

I'm not a big zombie shooter fan unless it's a co-op campaign which is why I was so excited about this game. There are very few true Co-Op campaign games in VR, and AZ sunshine was a blast I had hope this would be just as good...... It's not. Don't get me wrong it's fun, but the feeling of the guns and bullet system are beyond confusing. We spent an hour trying to get into a game together, the co-op execution could have been done so much better there's a certain way the host has to check ready before anyone else can and it disconnects a lot. If you don't make it through a section of the mission you have to do the entire mission all over again. If someone dies you have to hope you have a syringe no normal revive. There is no jump, and for the love of God why would you assign the eject button  on B instead of A.... Not to mention the satchel pop up coming up in the middle of a heated fight was beyond annoying. Doesn't matter where you're standing zombies can spawn right from underneath you. We have been playing on easy and cannot get past the second mission and now it's no longer fun, it could be, but it needs some updates. Add a story mode, have the ammunition box have all bullet types always or give players a choice of unlimited like zero caliber. Hopefully the devs listen to suggestions and continue to improve it. For example add a left holster slot to put melee or other weapons in and give us more room to put in our satchel. Something's got to give for this to be great......

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback, this will be passed on to the team. In terms of cooperative and matchmaking issues, this was a result of a large-scale internet and online services outage affecting lots of different companies. Sadly, it was poorly timed for launch and impacted Zombie Army VR matchmaking. We do apologise for the inconvenience and rest assured, this is now resolved.

reviews.review_description:

If you wanna know you’re the game you’re buying but overall it’s a great game love the story. It’s a great game. The goal is great. The Gore is great And just overall a great game.
I mind you should buy it, but read this first ￼ oh and quest two people do not buy this game. I feel like it’s better on quest three cause you get a better experience with the graphics and the physic. i’m just saying if you want a better experience I think quest three should be the people that buy it because if you want a great time and I’ll leave like one star review that I suggest you do it on the three all I am saying is that I think it’s really bad on the quest two because it’s a lot of graphics and Gore that a quest two cannot handle and it gets really buggy but on quest three it is better because the quest can handle more megabytes that it holds and it can handle the gore because it’s a better and with better arms, so I suggest you do not buy it on quest two￼

I got a different zombie game one time and you couldn't move or hit the zombies but this one is 5 stars. You can freely move and smack zombies with different types of weapons it's really a good amount of hard but overall a really really good game. MUST BUY
First game I've played in vr that has miss- assist ...

I wanted to love this game so much but wow is it rough. On a quest 3 it will crash randomly with a low memory error message. Visuals are very jittery and bland. Gameplay gets extremely repetitive very fast. Voice active is top notch.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you are experiencing this - could you pop some more info to our player support team? https://support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_description: This game sucks

So far, the game is awesome. Ever since I’ve played the first zombie army game, I’ve been a huge fan of the series since. And now that Rebellion has put their effort to make my favorite series of a game come to life in VR, I have never been excited as I am. Now, there is some lack of texture here and there, and a lack of blood when shooting the zombies, but their effort has earned my whole respect. The game does need more polishing, but it’ll be worth the wait.

So when I had saw this game coming to quest, my first thought was “ really? On vr is crazy” but when I played this, I was actually impressed! This game is soo fun haven’t beat it yet but so far it has really good gameplay and you can play multiplayer so it can feel like your playing COD zombies. This game isn’t the best game but ima give it a 4

Iam really enjoying the campaign and seems highly repayable but I really bought the game thinking there would be horde mode like the console games would be great if the devs could do one for this game I wouldn't mind paying for it

Got a few quirks but it was a very enjoyable game. Finished it on wussy mode. Now I’m replaying on manly mode. 
Good value for the price.
Hope they got a few add ons planned 
Will definitely purchase if they do

The problem i have with this game, is that it is a arcade shooter game, yes its better then the sniper game version more polishes controls ect. But for me the presentation, the few missions i did gave me the idea of playing a glorified on-rail shooter. A very weak and limited start.
I hunger for some good sneaking, zombie bashing, sniper action. Without all those limited game restrictions or dummed diwn controls.
Got some heavy mixed feeling over this.
4 stars not the less and i try it some more maybe it becomes better.

Huge fan of the Sniper Elite series and have been playing since the first SE on the original XBOX. I played this for about 3 hours lastnight and had an absolute blast. I do hope there is going to be more content added to keep people playing but as is I'm gonna be hooked to this for a while. Definitely worth checking out. Another great game from Rebellion!

reviews.review_title: Decent, could do with tweaks, still fun.
reviews.review_description:
Updated Review:

Having played through the game, I'm bumping up my rating. Mainly because despite its flaws the game is still fun to play, especially once you get into the swing of it.

One of the main criticisms I had concerning ammo availability tends not to be a big issue for most situations after all, just an early level where it is a bit ridiculous. 

I'm still critical of the levels themselves being such small spaces and as it turns out, the game sends you through many of the same maps from different ingress points too. Although it fits the narrative this is kinda disappointing. A bigger, better game is possible on this system but for what it is you can have a blast and it's a shame the devs couldn't give us something more polished to give it that wow factor.


********

Generally a decent game which I'll keep and play but there's a little list of things that bug me about it, especially as it was delayed.

The ammo to zombie ratio is kinda poor, unless you are situated near to a box. Even if you are, chances of getting the time to restock are slim to none given the way so many zombies are constantly spawned, especially in tight environments. 

There is nowhere to store a melee weapon so they're basically superfluous. If there was a slot for them, then you could at least fall back on them when you run dry and can try to fight to clear some room to stock up. Note, I don't include the ridiculously meagre 'backpack' with three spaces, I'm talking about a dedicated weapon slot which many other games provide.

Sometimes the ammo boxes don't stock all ammo types, only sniper ammo. If I have three weapons, I want to choose how and when to use them to slay zombies, not have my options restricted.

The barrel angle on the guns are off and there is no adjustment. Either the default needs to be brought down to a more sensible angle or the option to adjust added to the menu.

Whenever you use your off hand to stabilise a weapon, the weapon adopts the rotation angle of the off hand. Very off putting and requires unnecessary adjustment.

Animation on the x-ray cam is lacking, often looks like nothing is breaking and for some reason everything looks too small, like a tilt shift effect. Very weird.

Finally, based on what I've played so far the environments are small but it could be this changes throughout the campaign. However if it stays like that it will be a shame. No excuse to have small maps which you are practically funneled through in a modern game. And if your environments are small, you can afford a little more luxury on graphics.

reviews.review_title: Fun until it loses my progress
reviews.review_description: Really enjoy the game but twice now when I sign back on, the game loses my progress.  Not sure I want to start over again and keep going through this.   I was enjoying the action but it not saving my progress pisses me off.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're experiencing issues with save data. If you haven't done so already, please do be sure to reach out to our support team so they can investigate this for you: https://support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_title: Edited- Coop play now working perfectly
reviews.review_description:
Bought this game purposely for coop play and it does not work! When will this be fixed? Solo plays flawlessly upto yet On first mission but we need coop as game advertises having. 
I will of course give 5 star when fixed. 
Please hurry and activate servers on quest.

EDIT-
As promised. Here’s the 5 star review upgrade from 1 star. Large network outage was the cause unfortunately but now seemingly fixed thankfully. Not sure about this but either time got away from me and my girlfriend playing this, or the game is a bit of a battery zapper as we’ve had to come off prematurely to charge up lol. Can’t complain about this though really as this is quickly going to become one of our favourite games. So anyone after a AAA intense zombie shooter with lots of collectibles to store on display in your hub and lots of gore, explosions and zombies that surprising fire back at you. Then grab this game and don’t waste time getting up the leaderboards. 
Thanks to the developers for postponing the launch, it’s definately worth the wait.

developer_response.body: There is a large-scale network outage affecting a lot of online services at the moment. We're getting updates on it and hopefully it won't be long. Poor timing that it's landed on launch day. Sorry about the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: I was hoping I would be able to play with four of my friends because there is 4 people you can play as, despite there being 4 people you can only play with one other person. I am also disappointed because I have stick drift and I was trying to search in the settings for no stick turning but it doesn’t have that feature.

developer_response.body: Hopefully this will help solve your 'stick drift' issue - https://www.meta.com/en-gb/help/quest/5545675425557880/

reviews.review_title: Nice Zombie shooter, worth the price
reviews.review_description: It’s a fun game, nice zombie adventure. I’m liking it more after I put it on auto-reload & have gotten used to the controls. Give it a sec, you’ll dig it, especially as it gets further & further along.

Amazing work and great story, nothing really bad about it… it just needs a red dot 🔴 like lazer to make shooting easier, and add box like cod with random weapons that will be great, and a wave round that would brilliant 🤩 I’m enjoying it

Has more bugs than a shed with termites just changed my VR headset to a newer one won’t even load. Can’t even go to a help page to find out what’s going on. It’s absolutely ridiculous. I’m playing also more multiple games that are loading up fine on it, but zombie army again as the reports were put out that it’s buggy buggy buggy buggy and now I can’t even play and I pre-ordered it what an absolute awful game.￼

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear this, could you please contact our support team so they can look into any issues for you: https://support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_description: My husband and I regularly play games like After the Fall, Dungeons of Eternity,  etc. This is definitely one of the most fun games we play. We already beat it but are ready for more. One of the top few games we play, for sure.

Good game, its nothing insane, not top quality but you can get invested into the game pretty easily, funny moments and the upgrade system + all the easter eggs and badges makes it better incase you get a little bored, never in my life would i give it 5 stars, its just not that kind of game.
Also forewarning, gun upgrades don’t save, may have been my mistake but came back to the game with all my upgrades gone on my m1811 and tommy

developer_response.body: If the issue around gun upgrades persist, please reach out to player support: support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_title: fun-ish but under cooked and overpriced.
reviews.review_description:
first off, this isn't a bad game, 3 out of 5 is average, it's fun-ish, but it definitely feels rushed.

***edit*** dropping a star because a game at this price should be better - I would still rate three out of five if the only consideration was the game without considering the cost.***

So much of it feels half-finished. The mechanics, the levels, everything. Not terrible, just... I don't think it was laziness but it FEELS lazy.

a lot of this game has the DNA of something much better, and there is a lot of talent on display here. if only they were given the opportunity to flesh things out properly.

The game fails to differentiate between making something that challenges a player in an engaging way by testing their skills, and simply making things more difficult in a tedious way that can feel unfair at times.

in its current state, the game is obviously unfinished and not tested properly. It was probably rushed through development because of bad leadership and direction.

you can still play this game and have fun with it, it is a mostly fun game, just know it can feel frustrating and tedious especially as you progress past the halfway point.

it's a testament to the high quality of the development team that I was able to keep playing through the entire game without giving up, despite the blaring issues.

if you've already played through all the Arizona Sunshines for the fourth time and you have nothing else to play, I would recommend this game. just don't expect it to be as good as any of those.

it's the poor man's Arizona sunshine.

Edit: oh wait, this is a $30 game. at that price it has no excuse to be released in this state. I'm taking a star off for that out of principle. While the price doesn't affect The gameplay, it still has an effect on my ability to enjoy the game. I could have excused the current state of the game if it was under $20 or at least listed under Early Access.

reviews.review_title: Amazing even on quest 2but one complaint
reviews.review_description:
I adore this game, if ur into vr and liked the zombie army games then u have to play this. Thank u developers for making it work on quest 2. I can't afford to upgrade and love how this and the metro vr game still runs on a quest 2. And really well! I pre-ordered and have no regrets, ty ty for making it so well. Most quest games launch like garbage and this seem bug free so far.

Update: my one complaint. They reuse environments.....a lot. Maps are sometimes played in reverse in a different level. It can be a bit disappointing...... I would still recommend it, but it barely got that 5th star bc of this.

This game got a lot of hate even before it got released. I am so glad I didn’t listen to the “critics” because I’ve been having a blast with this game. Just pure fun to pick up and play. Totally recommend this title

It’s a ok game needs a little work ‘ for some reason I can’t get a multiplayer match ‘ but the game is good fun but it does get repetitive fast I have played more today and I will put my review upto four star as I managed to get a coop match and this makes playing in the harder difficulty much more fun there is one thing   when you are surrounded by zombies it gets stressful as guns jam and don’t load but after a few updates hopefully this gets fixed soon as I can see there is a lot of potential in this game and I think playing coop would be so much more fun also will there be more content added later on like more boss fights and zombie variety  as the game is very short 👍

developer_response.body:
There was n external, large-scale internet outage affecting online services yesterday which unfortunately fell on launch day and impacted matchmaking. This issue is now resolved. 

In terms of issues in-game with weapons jamming, please do reach out to player support so they can investigate this for you: https://support.rebellion.com/hc/en-gb/requests/new

reviews.review_title: Good game and getting better every updat
reviews.review_description:
Grip angle on pistols is atrocious and needs a setting in the menus for tuning. (Developers listened and added adjustments for grip angle!)

Highly recommend turning aim smoothing off so the game feels less clunky 

Good game and gameplay. Fun story

Killcam gets old after 20 headshots

So far I enjoy this game but it needs alil more polishing and the rift graphics are meh compared to older titles

I'm really enjoying Zombie Army VR, love zombie shooters.
Some things could be improved but it's still worth the money.

Devs if you happen to see this. Is this a feature or a bug. I'm was about half way through the game when I thought I didn't want to get to the end that quick so I went back to play any mission where I hadn't collected everything that was available.

When I went to the shooting range all my weapon upgrades had vanished, I still had the shields by each weapon. I couldn't re-earn upgrade kits because they were already collected.
When I went back to my previous position half way through the game my weapon upgrades were still all missing?

Devs, thanks for the update, that worked a treat all my upgrades where still available. I never thought of trying that where it said I had 0 upgrade kits.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the lovely review! In terms of your question, re-running the campaign from an earlier point, especially going back to the first level where you're assigned specific weapons for training, will result in your other weapons going back to their "base" state. The upgrades haven't been taken away or lost, you simply need to go to a work bench and reapply your desired loadout. This is expected behaviour. Hope that helps, Deadhunter! 🫡

reviews.review_title: ZOMBIE ARMY MIND KILLING BLOW! FIND OUT!
reviews.review_description: Hands down👐Solid gunplay🔫 and intense atmosphere!!🌚, It has me buzzing about replaybility▶️ and those juicy🍹 x-ray killcams!🦴
